title: Wii U
content:
Iwata Asks HomeWii U
Iwata Asks: Splatoon
1. It Started With Tofu2. Then Rabbits3. Got to be Squids4. Gaining Momentum5. This Depth Has No End6. Spell it Out in Black and White
It Started With TofuIwataYou芒聙聶re all wearing matching t-shirts.NogamiOh yes, we are. (laughs)SakaguchiIt was just a coincidence. (laughs)IwataIt doesn芒聙聶t look like a coincidence to me. (laughs)All(laughs)NogamiThere芒聙聶s actually a t-shirt like this in Splatoon. That芒聙聶s why we芒聙聶re all wearing them today.
1. Hisashi Nogami: Production Department, Entertainment Analysis & Development Division. Producer of Splatoon. Appeared in  Iwata Asks: Wii Channels 4: Aiming For Worldwide Mii Communication,  Iwata Asks: Animal Crossing: City Folk.IwataOh, is that so? Now, you first introduced Splatoon at last year芒聙聶s E32. What was the reaction like?
2. E3: Electronic Entertainment Expo. An industry event for computer and video games held every June in Los Angeles. Splatoon was presented for the first time at E3 in 2014.NogamiWe had always thought that it was a really fun game, but we were happy to get an even bigger reaction than we芒聙聶d expected. IwataWhy do you think it was so well received? NogamiI think it was because of how the game was easy to grasp once you start playing. Another part of it was that we revealed the game with a video. Amano-san studied filmmaking in college, so he made the announcement trailer for E3.IwataIt芒聙聶s not often that a trailer is made by one of the developers. Trailers are usually made by an outside agency.AmanoWe decided to make the video ourselves this time because we had a really clear vision of what we wanted to show and how we wanted to show it. I think the video gives you a good idea of the kind of game it is even without playing it. It was most important for us to show off our new squid character.
3. Yusuke Amano: Production Department, Entertainment Analysis & Development Division. Director of Splatoon, with Sakaguchi. Appeared in  Iwata Asks: Super Mario 25th Anniversary Volume 4: Super Mario Developers 2,  Iwata Asks: Star Fox 64 3D,  Iwata Asks: New Super Mario Bros. 2.SakaguchiI was at E3, and the media kept asking, "Why squid?"  (laughs)
4. Tsubasa Sakaguchi: Production Department, Entertainment Analysis & Development Division. Acted as Director on Splatoon, along with Amano. Appeared in  Iwata Asks: Nintendo Land.NogamiWe got asked that a lot. (laughs)IwataThe people reading this would probably also like us to start with the same question.NogamiRight. (laughs)IwataYou went through a lot of trial and error with this project, and ended up with squid. Let芒聙聶s start off talking about that.NogamiAlright. We started this project after I finished working on the launch of Wii U.IwataNogami-san, you worked on the menu for Wii U, right?NogamiRight. And Sakaguchi-san was involved in developing Nintendo Land5, and Sato-san...
5. Nintendo Land: A theme park game released for December 2012 for the Wii U. SatoI was developing New Super Mario Bros. U6.
6. New Super Mario Bros. U: An action game released for Wii U in December 2012.
7. Shintaro Sato: Production Department, Entertainment Analysis & Development Division. Program Director for Splatoon.NogamiSo, all of the team members were involved in the launch of Wii U. Once development had settled down, I gathered them all together and began this project. I said, "Let芒聙聶s make a new kind of game, without worrying about trying to fit into existing game genres." Inoue-san was also working on the Wii U menu, so I asked him to join the project.InoueThat芒聙聶s right.
8. Seita Inoue: Production Department, Entertainment Analysis & Development Division. Art Director for Splatoon.IwataSo you all got together to make a new kind of game, something that wasn芒聙聶t Super Mario Bros. or The Legend of Zelda.NogamiRight. Of course there were other members of the team who aren芒聙聶t here right now. All of us got together almost every day and brainstormed ideas for a new game. I think we had over fifty ideas...InoueWe had at least seventy ideas.IwataSeventy ideas? How long did it take you to think of that many?NogamiAbout six months.IwataThat芒聙聶s a lot of ideas.NogamiWe were all pretty competitive about our brainstorming.AmanoAnd sometimes we would be inspired by someone else芒聙聶s idea, and think, "Here芒聙聶s how I芒聙聶d do it, if I did that."IwataLike, "If that芒聙聶s what we芒聙聶re doing, I芒聙聶d do it this way."SakaguchiRight. Our ideas really branched out like that.NogamiSo we all brainstormed ideas and gave a few presentations, and the idea that made it all the way to the end was the idea that grew into Splatoon.IwataSo I have to ask...The Entertainment Analysis & Development Division9 where you all work is responsible for a lot of Nintendo芒聙聶s franchise characters, so they put a lot of work into maintaining series. This means that there芒聙聶s not often a chance to make something new.
9. Entertainment Analysis & Development Division: One of Nintendo芒聙聶s development divisions, headed by Shigeru Miyamoto. Responsible for the development of many of Nintendo芒聙聶s character driven titles such as Super Mario Bros., The Legend of Zelda, Animal Crossing and Pikmin.NogamiYou could say that.IwataOf course they made games with a new structure when the Wii System came out, but the last new character was Pikmin10 and that was about 14 years ago.
10. Pikmin: An AI action game series. The first game was released for the Nintendo GameCube in December, 2001. Pikmin 2 was released for Nintendo GameCube in August 2004, and Pikmin 3 was released for Wii U in August 2013.NogamiThat芒聙聶s right. That was the last new character from the Entertainment Analysis & Development Division (EAD), and it was made 14 years ago.AmanoBut in the beginning we weren芒聙聶t trying to create a new character. We were trying to create a new game structure first and foremost.NogamiThat芒聙聶s right. We weren芒聙聶t invested in creating a new character. We just set out to create a game with a new structure.IwataThat芒聙聶s very important. The way that (Shigeru) Miyamoto-san and the EAD make games, the idea comes not from the design, but from the function. The design comes after.
NogamiThat芒聙聶s right.IwataSo you don芒聙聶t approach making a game because you want to use a certain character.NogamiRight.IwataSo what was the idea that became the basis for Splatoon? NogamiA demo made by our Program Director Sato-san. At first there was a white cube in a maze...
IwataTofu?NogamiYes. (laughs) There was a white thing and a black thing shaped like blocks of tofu, and they were shooting ink and they had to steal each other芒聙聶s turf.  IwataSo at first it wasn芒聙聶t squid at all. It was black and white tofu shooting each other with ink. (laughs)NogamiRight. (laughs)IwataBut tofu is usually white. SakaguchiThe black one was sesame tofu! (laughs)SatoSo the basis of Splatoon was a fight between a block of sesame tofu and a block of firm tofu.All(laugh)
